preview.summary: Co-hosts USA face a youthful Australia in a pivotal Group D clash, with a partisan Seattle crowd behind Mauricio Pochettino's side. The Socceroos will look to frustrate a USA attack led by Christian Pulisic and spring chances on the break.
preview.homeForm: The USA carry home advantage as co-hosts, with a roster blending 13 returnees from their 2022 round-of-16 run and 13 World Cup debutants.
preview.awayForm: Australia, ranked 27th, reached a sixth straight World Cup after finishing second to Japan in Asian qualifying.
preview.homeKey: Christian Pulisic, most-capped Milan forward and attacking talisman.
preview.awayKey: Mathew Ryan, veteran keeper and captain at his fourth World Cup.
storylines: Pochettino's hosts under pressure to deliver on home soil
Australia's 17 debutants step onto the biggest stage
Nestory Irankunda's pace a fresh Socceroos threat
preview.h2h: The two have met just three times since 2010, the USA winning twice including a 2-1 victory in October 2025, and have never met at a World Cup.
